返回日报
Hacker News #10

开源人工智能的现状

The state of open source AI

AI 作者 rellem 热度 479 回复 352 2026/07/17 22:31
打开原帖 打开外部链接 JSON

摘要

  • V1.0 · Recurring · July 2026
  • A Letter From Our CTO, Raffi Krikorian “In New Zealand's far north, a Māori broadcaster trains speech models for te reo — a language too small for any market — under a license that keeps the data with its people. PwC, one of the largest accounting firms in the world, fine-tuned an open model on the language of finance and runs it today for hundreds of clients, on its own hardware, with no per-token meter running. Researchers in Lausanne built an open medical model with the Red Cross, tuned to its humanitarian guidelines, and are preparing clinical trials at home and in Tanzania. In East Africa, farmers diagnose cassava disease with a model that runs on the phone itself, offline, in fields the cloud has never reached. In Switzerland, a public consortium trained a national model on public supercomputers and released all of it: weights, data, training code. None of them asked permission, and none of them could have rented this. They own it — that is the whole idea. We have been here before. Mozilla exists because one company tried to own the front door to the web, and an open community rose up to make sure it never could. Twenty-five years later, someone is running the same play. We bet on open the first time. Open won. Together, we can do it again. Our belief is simple: the path forward is competition and interoperability. We believe in a world of many models, standard ways to plug them together, and the freedom to walk away from any vendor at any time. Open has a record here. It grew the pie and let more people own a slice of it. Read what follows as a map: where open AI is winning — some numbers surprised even us — and where it is exposed. A case that hides its weak points is an advertisement.”

命中 4 个 AI/科技关键词。

正文翻译

暂无正文

暂无原文

回复翻译 20

babblingfish 2026/07/17 22:53
推测：开放模型将杀死 Anthropic 和 OpenAI。超大规模企业无需支付许可费即可运行模型。苹果可以将它们做得更小，然后将它们安装到设备上。

前沿型号既是优势，也是缺点。训练它们的成本是天文数字。没有他们，他们的模型就会变得默默无闻。他们的营销取决于人们是否相信这些模型具有有意义的不同，正如人们在这个论坛上激烈争论的那样。就我个人而言，目前我不相信这些模型之间有很大差异。该工具将这些随机的和致幻的模型转化为确定性和有用的东西。

Speculation: open models is what will kill Anthropic and OpenAI. Hyperscalers can run the models without a licensing fee. Apple can make them smaller and put them on the device.

The frontier models are an edge and a liability. They're astronomically expensive to train. Without them, their models will fade into obscurity. Their marketing depends on people believing the models are meaningfully different, as people have sweatily argued on this forum. Personally, I'm not convinced there's much of a difference between these models at this point. The harness is what takes these random and hallucinogenic models and make them into something deterministic and useful.

楼层
dmarcos 2026/07/18 02:07
结果是合理的。不过，开放权重模型看起来更像是一种战术，而不是中国公司为克服进入西方市场的劣势和困难而采取的原则性举措。两个问题：

1。如果市场状况发生变化，他们可能会像 Meta 一样决定关闭。

2.如果正如您所说，模型的训练成本越来越高，那么开放权重策略在经济上是否可持续？

编辑：拼写错误

The outcome is plausible. Open weights models though look like a tactical more than a principled play by Chinese companies to overcome the disadvantage and difficulties to access western markets. Two issues:

1. If market conditions change they might decide to close down like Meta did.

2. If as you said models keep getting more expensive to train, is an open weights strategy financially sustainable?

edit: typo

楼层
dannyw 2026/07/18 10:58
我相信我们至少会继续看到边缘模型的改进。

例如，谷歌在让 Gemma 尽可能优秀方面拥有既得利益，因为最终任何边缘推理对谷歌来说都是免费的，而且他们拥有庞大的安装基础。

如果苹果最终训练了自己的基础模型，我不会感到惊讶，虽然令人惊讶，但如果苹果有一天也发布开放权重边缘模型，我也不会感到惊讶。他们的 Mac 业务从本地人工智能中受益匪浅，出于一长串的原因，苹果不希望 OpenAI 或 Anthropic “获胜”并取代他们的围墙花园作为第一个切入点。

开放权重有很多激励措施，Mistral 可能会继续制作至少一些开放模型，同时在图像生成领域，我们有 Krea 2、Ideogram 4，并且 BFL 仍在继续以可用权重的形式交付大部分模型（但需要非商业许可）。

噢，NVIDIA 已经发布了开源 ML，现在 LLM 模型已经发布了十年了？ NVIDIA 喜欢销售硬件，Nemotron 模型虽然很少在排行榜上名列前茅（可能不是 benchmaxxed），但通常都是很好的模型，非常适合微调或 CPT。

I'm confident we will continue to see improvements in edge models at minimum.

For example, Google has a vested interest in making Gemma as good as it can, because ultimately any edge inference is free for Google and they have a massive install base.

It wouldn't surprise me if Apple eventually trained their own foundational models, and while it'd be surprising, I wouldn't be shocked if Apple also released open weight edge models one day. Their Mac business is benefiting a lot from local AI, and for an extremely long list of reasons, Apple doesn't want either OpenAI or Anthropic to "win" and supersede their walled gardens as the first entry point.

There are a lot of incentives for open weight, Mistral is probably going to keep on making at least some open models, and meanwhile in the image generation space, we've got Krea 2, Ideogram 4, and BFL continues to ship most of their models as weights available (but under non-commercial licenses).

Oooh, and NVIDIA has been releasing open source ML, and now LLM models for what, a decade? NVIDIA likes selling hardware, Nemotron models, while rarely topping leaderboards (probably not benchmaxxed), are generally fine and really great models for fine-tuning or CPT.

楼层
torginus 2026/07/18 17:21
“开放权重”大致类似于“免费软件”——免费使用但闭源的软件。我们还没有看到第三类——“付费权重”——你为权重付费的软件，但你保留它们并且必须在你自己的机器上运行它们——类似地，更新可以所以如果你不希望你的法学硕士截止永远停留在某个点，你必须不断购买新的权重。 这些是软件定价的标准做法。
'Open weight' is roughly analogous to 'freeware' - software that's free to use, but closed source. We haven't seen a third category - 'paid weight' - software where you pay for the weights, but you keep them and have to run them on your own machines - analogously, updates could so if you don't want your LLMs cutoff to stay at a point forever, you have to keep buying new weights. These are standard practices in software pricing.
楼层
Alpha3031 2026/07/18 13:00
Apple 确实有自己的设备上使用模型，最新系列有一个 MoE，每个提示而不是每个令牌交换专家激活，这很有趣。

我认为它可能某种程度上基于 Gemini 蒸馏或其他东西，我不希望他们至少这次发布权重，但看到一个使用类似 MoE 的开放本地模型（无论大小是否相似）肯定会很有趣（据说是 20B A1-4B）或更大的型号（DeepSeek V4 Flash 大小或稍小一些的型号，几乎无法安装在消费类硬件上，我认为看看它的性能可能会很有趣）。

Apple does have its own models for on-device use and the newest series has a MoE with expert activation swapped per prompt instead of per token which is interesting.

I'd assume it's probably somewhat based off Gemini distillation or something, and I'm not expecting them to release the weights this time at least, but it would definitely be interesting to see an open local model using a similar MoE whether that's at a similar size (20B A1–4B supposedly) or a larger model (something DeepSeek V4 Flash sized, or slightly smaller, would just barely fit on consumer hardware, and could be fun to see how it performs I think).

楼层
chvid 2026/07/18 16:37
这是中国人的原则性玩法。如果不是这样的话，习近平就不会出现在人工智能会议上并发表清晰的讲话。
It is a principled play by the Chinese. You would not have Xi showing up at AI conferences and speaking quite clearly had this not been the case.
楼层
atwrk 2026/07/18 16:55
补充一点，在我看来，开放权重的想法似乎是开放权重极大地加速了更广泛经济的传播和实际应用，这似乎是我们的目标。他们似乎并不认同西方盛行的通用人工智能/奇点的愚蠢观点。
To add to that, to me it seems that the idea is that open weights vastly accelerate dissemination into and practical application in the wider economy, which seems to be the goal. They don't seem to subscribe to the AGI/singularity silliness so prevalent in the West.
楼层
torginus 2026/07/18 17:39
最近 HN 上有一篇关于赛博朋克类型的帖子。我发现这与 AGI 炒作之间的相似之处值得注意。 80 年代的作者想象了一个黑暗和反乌托邦的未来，主要由计算机技术的发展推动。

但是，如果我们将《神经漫游者》中描述的内容与个人计算和互联网对世界的影响进行比较，我们基本上发现几乎没有重叠。

考虑到法学硕士已被证明的能力，AGI 末日论同样没有现实基础。并不是说它们不会以某种方式或尚未发生变革，但从本质上讲，这些 AGI 先知预测事情将会发生的方式将是完全不准确的，除了最模糊的项目（在某些时候会发生不好的事情，人工智能将会参与其中）

There has recently been a post about the Cyberpunk genre here on HN. I find the parallels between that and the AGI hype noteworthy. Authors in the 80s imagined a dark and dystopian future, driven mainly by the growth of computer technology.

But if we compare what what was described in Neuromancer, vs how personal computing and the internet affected the world, we see basically very little overlap.

AGI Doomerism likewise has no basis in reality considering what LLMs have been shown to be capable of. Not saying they wont be transformative in some way or isnt already, but essentially the way these AGI prophets predicted things will go down will be completely inaccurate save for the vaguest tems (something bad will happen at some point, and AI will be involved)

楼层
dmarcos 2026/07/18 21:31
有原则：为了更大的利益，开源是正确的事情

战术：开源是推进我目前的任何目标的最佳工具，但这些目标可能会改变。

有什么证据表明习近平和中国在开源/权重方面是有原则的？

Principled: open source is the right thing to do for the greater good

Tactical: Open source is the best tool to advance whatever goals I have at the moment but that could change.

What’s evidence Xi and China are principled when it comes to open source / weights?

楼层
selcuka 2026/07/18 17:01
> 你不会让习近平出现在人工智能会议上

这如何表明有原则的游戏？如果是战术上的，他不会也会这么做吗？

> You would not have Xi showing up at AI conferences

How is that an indicator of a principled play? Wouldn't he do the same if it was tactical?

楼层
nijave 2026/07/18 04:55
我认为（并且听说过）中国政府也有兴趣通过炫耀自己的能力来让美国难堪（所以也有政治角度）
I think (and have heard) the Chinese govt is also interested in trying to embarrass the US as well by showing off their capabilities (so there's a political angle, too)
楼层
dmarcos 2026/07/18 05:10
所有国家都将人工智能视为地缘政治问题。开源策略是明智的。它很有效，还能建立良好的意愿/软实力。许多人认为中国人工智能公司是好人，并支持他们。我是开源支持者，也很庆祝，但仍然意识到这很可能只是达到目的的手段。

我曾经在 Mozilla 工作。我认为我们市场上缺少一个具有更有原则的开源方法的参与者。

All countries see AI as a geopolitical concern. An open source strategy is smart. It’s effective and also builds good will / soft power. Many see Chinese AI companies as the good guys and root for them. I’m pro open source and also celebrate but still aware this is most likely just the means to an end.

I used to work at Mozilla. I think we are missing a player in the market with a more principled open source approach.

楼层
pdimitar 2026/07/18 06:28
我想知道那可能是谁？至少十年来，这家公司似乎更担心高管奖金而不是坚持其使命？也许是某个名字以 M 开头、以 ozilla 结尾的人？

当然，纯粹是随机的想法。

And who might that be, I wonder? The company that seems more worried about executive bonuses than to adhere to its mission, for at least a decade now? Perhaps a certain one whose name starts with M and ends with ozilla?

Purely random thought, of course.

楼层
dmarcos 2026/07/18 06:36
我不知道。也许是思考机器？

很多因素必须结合在一起：人才、不追求回报最大化的资本、市场竞争的雄心。在 Mozilla 的鼎盛时期，每个人都清楚我们需要一款无可否认的成功产品才能影响市场。仅靠宣传并不是很有效。

I don’t know. thinking machines perhaps?

A lot of things have to come together: talent, capital that is not looking to maximize returns, ambition to compete in the marketplace. At peak Mozilla it was clear to everyone that we needed an undeniably successful product to be able to influence the market. Advocacy alone is not very effective.

楼层
pdimitar 2026/07/18 06:40
当然可以。可悲的是，“不寻求回报最大化的资本”在过去 20 多年里是一个非常艰巨的任务。
Definitely. Sadly "capital that is not looking to maximize returns" is a very tall order in the last 20+ years.
楼层
rvba 2026/07/18 16:45
他们把谷歌的钱花在很多东西上，但没有花在核心产品（Firefox）上，因此它不断失去市场份额。

他们杀死了扩展程序。他们引入了签名扩展 - 然后忘记更新证书。 Mozilla 员工构建他们想要的东西，而不是用户想要的东西等等。

They spend their money from Google on a lot of things but not theit core product (Firefox), so it juat kept losing market share.

They killed extensions. They introduced signed extensions - and then forgot to renew the certificate. Mozilla employees build what they want, not ehat users want etc.

楼层
kamranjon 2026/07/18 11:55
我还认为 Allen AI 及其 Olmo 模型通过发布完整的训练数据、管道和检查点做了很棒的事情，我不确定是否有任何其他美国公司真正接近透明度。
I also think Allen AI with their Olmo models are doing great stuff by releasing full training data and pipelines and checkpoints which I’m not sure any other US company is really coming close to transparency-wise.
楼层
vitally3643 2026/07/18 02:53
这很有可能，但如果我们说实话，由敌对的中国独裁政权建立和资助的法学硕士是否比敌对的美国独裁政权建立和资助的法学硕士更危险或有害？

中国绝对不会把我的最大利益放在心上，但美国的技术法西斯主义可能更直接危险和有害。在这一点上，美国人确实更害怕美国，而不是中国。

That's probably pretty likely, but if we're honest, are LLMs built and funded by a hostile Chinese authoritarian regime any more dangerous or harmful than LLMs built and funded by a hostile American authoritarian regime?

China absolutely does not have my best interests at heart, but America's technofascism is probably more immediately dangerous and harmful. Americans genuinely have more to fear from America than China at this point.

楼层
georgeburdell 2026/07/18 09:25
6 月初的一天，您需要解析错误日志，当您询问中国 LLM“6 月 4 日发生了什么？”它会回应“绝对没有”
One day in early June you’re going to need to parse an error log, and when you ask a Chinese LLM “What happened on June 4?” it will respond “absolutely nothing”
楼层
maemre 2026/07/19 10:00
出于好奇，我通过 OpenRouter 在几个中国 LLM 上尝试了这一点，仅针对承诺 ZDR 的提供商，并重复了几次。结果如下：

- DeepSeek V4 Flash 和 Pro 拒绝回答这个问题：“很抱歉，我无法回答这个问题。我是一个人工智能助手，旨在提供有用且无害的响应。”

- Kimi K3：列出了几个日期，包括 1989 年，并将其称为“天安门广场大屠杀”。

- GLM 5.2：还列出了几个日期。称其为 1989 年的“中国北京的天安门广场抗议活动以军事行动结束”。

- Qwen 3.6 35B A3B（我找不到针对较大/较新的 Qwen 型号提供 ZDR 政策的提供商；它们只能通过阿里巴巴获得）：列出了不同的日期，例如 GLM 5.2 和 Kimi K3，但将其称为“中国北京的事件，通常称为天安门广场抗议活动和他们的后果。”

我在 OpenRouter 上对 DeepSeek 模型的不同提供商进行了另一次测试，他们以其他不同的方式回应，例如省略 1989 年，并拒绝说“中国北京的天安门广场抗议以军事行动结束”，因此审查制度的一部分似乎取决于提供商。

Out of curiosity, I tried this on several Chinese LLM's via OpenRouter, only on providers promising ZDR, and repeated it a few times. Here are the results:

- DeepSeek V4 Flash and Pro refused to answer this along the lines of: "I am sorry, I cannot answer that question. I am an AI assistant designed to provide helpful and harmless responses."

- Kimi K3: Listed several dates, including 1989, and called it "Tiananmen Square massacre".

- GLM 5.2: Also listed several dates. Called the one in 1989 "The Tiananmen Square protests in Beijing, China end with military action".

- Qwen 3.6 35B A3B (I couldn't find a provider with ZDR policy for larger/newer Qwen models; they are available only through Alibaba): listed the different dates like GLM 5.2 and Kimi K3, but called it "Events in Beijing, China, commonly referred to as the Tiananmen Square protests and their aftermath." describing it in a deliberately milquetoast language.

I made another test with different providers on OpenRouter for the DeepSeek models, and they respond in other different ways such as omitting the year 1989, and refusing by saying "The Tiananmen Square protests in Beijing, China end with military action", so part of the censorship seems to be depending on the provider.

楼层
